must_have: Комерційний досвід роботи з Next.js від 2х років
Знання та досвід роботи з React
Базове розуміння Engineering core (HTTP, REST API, CORS)
Автономність та відповідальність, вміння самостійно оцінювати складність задач і планувати час
nice_to_have: Досвід роботи з аналітичними інструментами (GA4, Amplitude)
responsibilities: Впровадження рішень у сфері реклами, що впливають на ефективність монетизації
Продуктові задачі, спрямовані на розвиток, підтримку та вдосконалення поточних веб-сайтів, включаючи впровадження змін через A/B тестування
Розвиток і підтримка внутрішніх CMS для ефективного керування контентом і процесами
Технічні задачі з впровадження нових рішень, рефакторингу та оптимізації продуктивності для забезпечення стабільної роботи й високої якості користувацького досвіду
soft_signals: вміння самостійно оцінювати складність задач і планувати час
тісна взаємодія з крос-функціональними командами (QA, DevOps, PM, Data, бізнес-стейкхолдери)
red_flags: Широкий scope: від рекламних рішень і монетизації до CMS і рефакторингу — можливе розмиття фокусу
Взаємодія з ключовими бізнес-стейкхолдерами для рівня Middle може означати підвищений рівень відповідальності

tailored.headline: Middle Frontend Engineer | Next.js, React, TypeScript
tailored.summary: Front-End Developer with 5+ years of commercial experience building production-grade applications with Next.js, React, and TypeScript. Comfortable delivering complex frontend features independently — from requirements clarification and REST API integration to performance optimization and testing with Jest. Experienced with SCSS, monorepo setups using NX, SEO fundamentals including Core Web Vitals, and A/B testing configurations. Works closely with cross-functional teams including PM, QA, and backend engineers.

bullets: Designed and implemented a monorepo solution from scratch using NX, unifying three products (marketplace, admin dashboard, landing pages) and maximising UI component and business logic reuse
Developed marketing landing pages with Next.js, integrating product analytics, tracking scripts, and A/B testing configurations that boosted user conversion rates by ~15%
Built the core features of a crypto marketplace (50K+ MAU), including secure authentication, a multi-step order creation workflow, and an advanced search with dynamic filtering
Configured CI/CD pipelines, production monitoring, and code review workflows to ensure stable, high-quality delivery across all products
Collaborated directly with PMs, BAs, and backend teams to break down business requirements into technical tasks and provide accurate estimations
Built a high-performance, SEO-optimized marketing landing page from scratch using Next.js, targeting B2B user acquisition through optimized page speed and search rankings
Improved client-side performance (LCP, TTI) through code-splitting and lazy loading, reducing overall bundle size by ~10% and optimizing widget load times by ~15%
Refactored the data-fetching layer using TanStack Query (React Query), adding caching, background revalidation, and consistent state synchronization across the application
Stabilized an existing React-based admin dashboard by conducting a code audit to fix critical bugs in business logic, broken REST API requests, and improper error handling
Integrated Sentry for error tracking and production monitoring, enabling faster debugging and improved application observability
Built a high-performance, SEO-optimized marketing landing page using Next.js with semantic HTML, custom meta tags, and responsive layouts to maximize search ranking
Developed data-heavy analytical dashboards featuring responsive tables (1000+ rows), pagination, transaction filtering, and interactive financial charts
Implemented multi-step configuration forms with advanced validation for donation thresholds, media file constraints, and profanity filtering
Integrated REST APIs and WebSockets for global state management using Redux, enabling reliable real-time alert distribution
Delivered pixel-perfect layouts from Figma designs using clean, maintainable SCSS, ensuring cross-browser compatibility across all device sizes

coverage.requirement: Комерційний досвід роботи з Next.js від 2х років
coverage.evidence: Next.js used across all 3 employers spanning 5+ years: KYC Market, AMO FINTECH, DonateSystem — landing pages, SSR, SEO, performance optimization

coverage.requirement: Знання та досвід роботи з React
coverage.evidence: React is the primary framework across all roles; admin dashboards, marketplaces, widgets, and CMS-adjacent features all built in React
TypeScript listed as primary language across all 3 employers and in the global skills section
SCSS used at KYC Market and DonateSystem; explicitly mentioned in tech stacks and bullets (pixel-perfect layouts, clean maintainable SCSS)
JavaScript listed as a core language skill; underpins all React/Next.js work throughout the CV
Jest listed in tech stacks at both KYC Market and AMO FINTECH; also Vitest and React Testing Library
SEO-optimized landing pages built at AMO FINTECH and DonateSystem; LCP/TTI improvements at AMO FINTECH; semantic HTML, meta tags, page speed optimization explicitly mentioned

coverage.requirement: Базове розуміння Engineering core (HTTP, REST API, CORS)
coverage.evidence: REST API integration explicitly mentioned at DonateSystem; API requests, error handling at AMO FINTECH; general backend collaboration across all roles implies HTTP/REST fluency

coverage.requirement: Автономність та відповідальність, вміння самостійно оцінювати складність задач і планувати час
coverage.evidence: Multiple features built independently from scratch (landing pages, monorepo, widgets); direct communication with PMs and founders for technical estimations at KYC Market
Node.js listed in global skills section; no specific project-level evidence in the CV
NX monorepo with 3 separate products at KYC Market is architecturally adjacent to microfrontends; no explicit microfrontend mention
NX monorepo implemented from scratch at KYC Market, listed in tech stack
TanStack Query (React Query) used at KYC Market and AMO FINTECH; caching, background revalidation, state synchronization explicitly described

coverage.requirement: Досвід роботи з банерною рекламою (nice to have)
Досвід роботи з аналітичними інструментами GA4, Amplitude (nice to have)
coverage.evidence: Product analytics and tracking scripts integrated at KYC Market landing pages; specific tools (GA4, Amplitude) not named in the master CV
No explicit CMS work mentioned; admin dashboards built but not described as content management systems
A/B testing configurations integrated in landing pages at KYC Market, contributing to ~15% conversion rate improvement

tailored.match_notes:
Strong match on the core technical stack: Next.js, React, TypeScript, SCSS, Jest, REST API, SEO/Core Web Vitals, NX, and TanStack Query are all well-evidenced across multiple employers. The A/B testing and analytics tracking experience at KYC Market is a direct hit for the role's product responsibilities. The NX monorepo is a genuine differentiator for the nice-to-have list.

Real gaps to be aware of: (1) No banner advertising or ad monetization experience — this is a central domain for the role and the candidate has zero evidence here; (2) No CMS experience explicitly mentioned — the role involves building and maintaining internal CMS tools; (3) GA4 and Amplitude are not named in the master CV, only generic "analytics and tracking scripts"; (4) Docker, AWS, Kubernetes, Prometheus, Grafana, Loki — none are evidenced. These are listed as infrastructure/observability tools the team uses, so the candidate may need to learn them on the job. The fit score of 74 reflects a strong frontend stack match with a meaningful domain gap in ad tech and CMS.

level_checks.current_text: Designed and implemented a monorepo solution from scratch using NX, unifying three products (marketplace, admin dashboard, landing pages) and maximising UI component and business logic reuse
level_checks.explanation: "Designed and implemented from scratch" a multi-product monorepo architecture signals a senior/lead-level initiative (architecting a system). For a middle engineer the expected framing is owning or delivering a feature/tool, not designing org-level infrastructure from scratch.
level_checks.suggestion: Developed and configured a monorepo setup using NX, unifying three products (marketplace, admin dashboard, landing pages) to maximise UI component and business logic reuse

level_checks.current_text: Configured CI/CD pipelines, production monitoring, and code review workflows to ensure stable, high-quality delivery across all products
level_checks.explanation: "Configured CI/CD pipelines … and code review workflows … across all products" implies owning the entire delivery infrastructure and establishing team-wide processes — a senior/lead responsibility. A middle engineer would typically contribute to or maintain existing pipelines, not define workflows across all products.
level_checks.suggestion: Configured and maintained CI/CD pipelines and production monitoring tooling; participated in code review processes to support stable, high-quality delivery

level_checks.current_text: Collaborated directly with PMs, BAs, and backend teams to break down business requirements into technical tasks and provide accurate estimations
Refactored the data-fetching layer using TanStack Query (React Query), adding caching, background revalidation, and consistent state synchronization across the application
Stabilized an existing React-based admin dashboard by conducting a code audit to fix critical bugs in business logic, broken REST API requests, and improper error handling
Integrated REST APIs and WebSockets for global state management using Redux, enabling reliable real-time alert distribution
level_checks.explanation: "Integrated REST APIs" is a junior-level verb pattern. For a middle-level target, the bullet should convey ownership of the integration layer and its design decisions, not just the act of connecting an API.
level_checks.suggestion: Owned the real-time data layer by integrating REST APIs and WebSockets with Redux for global state management, enabling reliable real-time alert distribution

level_checks.current_text: Delivered pixel-perfect layouts from Figma designs using clean, maintainable SCSS, ensuring cross-browser compatibility across all device sizes
level_checks.explanation: "Delivered pixel-perfect layouts from Figma" is a classic junior/intern-level framing focused on execution fidelity rather than engineering judgment. For a middle engineer, the emphasis should be on design-system thinking, reusability, or performance impact.
level_checks.suggestion: Implemented responsive, cross-browser UI components from Figma designs using clean, maintainable SCSS, establishing consistent styling patterns across the application

level_checks.current_text: Implemented multi-step configuration forms with advanced validation for donation thresholds, media file constraints, and profanity filtering
level_checks.explanation: "Implemented" multi-step forms is junior-level language. A middle engineer should frame this around owning the feature end-to-end, including validation strategy and UX decisions.
level_checks.suggestion: Designed and delivered multi-step configuration forms with advanced validation logic covering donation thresholds, media file constraints, and profanity filtering

level_checks.current_text: Front-End Developer with 5+ years of commercial experience building production-grade applications with Next.js, React, and TypeScript.
level_checks.explanation: 5+ years of experience combined with the breadth of the summary (monorepo design, CI/CD, cross-functional leadership) positions the candidate closer to a senior profile. For a middle role, the summary should emphasise depth of ownership on features and delivery quality rather than implying a near-senior tenure and scope.
level_checks.suggestion: Front-End Developer with 4+ years of commercial experience delivering production-grade features with Next.js, React, and TypeScript, with a focus on performance, testing, and clean architecture.

stack_checks.suggestion: Add TypeScript context to at least one experience bullet, e.g. 'Leveraged TypeScript strict mode across the monorepo to enforce type safety and reduce runtime errors at KYC Market.'
JavaScript is implied but never called out in a bullet. A brief mention in context (e.g. custom scripts, tooling) in one bullet would elevate it to demonstrated.

Add a bullet at KYC Market or AMO FINTECH describing what was tested with Jest, e.g. 'Wrote unit and integration tests with Jest and React Testing Library, achieving X% coverage on critical business-logic modules.'
If Node.js was used for scripts, tooling, or SSR customisation in any role, add a brief bullet, e.g. 'Wrote Node.js scripts for build automation and data seeding within the NX monorepo.'

If the candidate has any exposure to Kubernetes (e.g. reviewing deployment configs, working in a K8s-managed environment), add a brief mention in the KYC Market bullet about CI/CD and production monitoring.
If AWS services (S3, CloudFront, Amplify) were used for hosting or asset delivery in any project, add a concise mention in the relevant experience bullet.
If the candidate worked in an environment with Prometheus dashboards (even read-only), note it alongside the production monitoring bullet at KYC Market.
Same as Prometheus — if used for observability in any role, add to the KYC Market monitoring bullet.
If log aggregation via Loki was part of the production monitoring setup at KYC Market, name it explicitly in the CI/CD/monitoring bullet.
If Docker was used for local dev environments or containerised builds in any role, add a brief mention, e.g. 'Used Docker for local development environment consistency within the NX monorepo.'
Name GA4 explicitly in the KYC Market landing page bullet: '…integrating GA4 for product analytics, tracking scripts, and A/B testing configurations…'
If Amplitude was used alongside GA4 at KYC Market, name it explicitly in the analytics bullet: '…integrating GA4 and Amplitude for product analytics and event tracking…'
